questions.q: The Rani of Jhansi Regiment was a unit of which larger force?
questions.explanation: The Rani of Jhansi Regiment was the women's combat unit of the Indian National Army.

questions.q: What made the Rani of Jhansi Regiment distinctive among military units of its time?
options: It was made up only of children
It was an all-women combat unit
questions.explanation: The regiment was notable as an all-women combat unit, a rarity in Asia at the time.

questions.q: Who commanded the Rani of Jhansi Regiment?
questions.explanation: The regiment was led by Captain Lakshmi Sahgal, also known as Lakshmi Swaminathan.

questions.q: In which year was the Rani of Jhansi Regiment formed?
questions.explanation: The Rani of Jhansi Regiment was formed in 1943 under the Indian National Army.

questions.q: After which historical figure was the Rani of Jhansi Regiment named?
questions.explanation: The regiment was named after Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi, the warrior queen of the 1857 revolt.

questions.q: Which INA leader inspired and authorized the creation of the all-women Rani of Jhansi Regiment?
questions.explanation: Subhas Chandra Bose championed the formation of the women's regiment within the INA.

questions.q: The Rani of Jhansi Regiment was recruited mainly from Indian women living where?
questions.explanation: Recruits came largely from Indian expatriate communities in Southeast Asia, such as Malaya and Burma.

questions.q: What was the broad purpose of the Rani of Jhansi Regiment?
questions.explanation: Like the rest of the INA, the regiment was raised to fight for India's freedom from British rule.

questions.q: The Rani of Jhansi Regiment is often described as among the earliest organized women's units of its kind in which continent?
questions.explanation: It was among the earliest organized all-women military formations of its kind in Asia.

questions.q: Captain Lakshmi Sahgal, who led the regiment, was trained in which profession before joining the INA?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi Sahgal was a qualified doctor practising in Singapore before she joined the INA.

questions.q: In which city was the Rani of Jhansi Regiment first established in 1943?
questions.explanation: The regiment was formally inaugurated in Singapore in 1943, where the INA had its headquarters.

questions.q: The recruits of the Rani of Jhansi Regiment were drawn especially from women working in which industry of Malaya?
questions.explanation: Many recruits came from families of Indian labourers on the rubber and tea plantations of Malaya.

questions.q: What month and year did Subhas Chandra Bose formally announce the formation of the women's regiment?
questions.explanation: Bose announced the formation of the Rani of Jhansi Regiment in July 1943 in Singapore.

questions.q: By what shorter name was the Rani of Jhansi Regiment commonly referred to?
options: The Rani Jhansi Regiment or RJR
questions.explanation: The unit was commonly called the Rani Jhansi Regiment, abbreviated RJR.

questions.q: What kind of training did members of the Rani of Jhansi Regiment receive?
options: Military and combat training including arms drill
questions.explanation: The women received genuine military training, including weapons handling and combat drill, not merely support roles.

questions.q: The Rani of Jhansi Regiment was part of the INA's broader push toward India during which 1944 campaign?
options: The advance toward the Burma-India frontier
questions.explanation: The regiment moved with the INA toward Burma during the 1944 advance against the British on India's frontier.

questions.q: What happened to the Rani of Jhansi Regiment after Japan's surrender in 1945?
options: It was disbanded and members made their way home
questions.explanation: With the INA's collapse in 1945, the regiment was disbanded and its members dispersed homeward.

questions.q: Captain Lakshmi Sahgal also briefly held what kind of post in the Provisional Government of Free India?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi Sahgal served in the Azad Hind provisional government with responsibility for women's organisation.

questions.q: The regiment's creation reflected Bose's broader belief in what?
options: Keeping women out of public life
Limiting the army to volunteers from India only
The active participation of women in the freedom struggle
questions.explanation: Forming the women's regiment expressed Bose's conviction that women should take an active combat role in the struggle.

questions.q: Long after independence, Lakshmi Sahgal remained active in public life and in 2002 did what?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi Sahgal stood as a candidate in India's 2002 presidential election, a measure of her enduring public stature.

questions.q: On which date in 1943 did Subhas Chandra Bose announce his intention to raise the women's regiment in a Singapore speech?
questions.explanation: Bose announced the plan to form the women's regiment in a speech in Singapore on 9 July 1943.

questions.q: Roughly how many women are generally estimated to have enrolled in the Rani of Jhansi Regiment at its strength?
questions.explanation: Estimates of the regiment's enrolment vary, but it is commonly placed at over a thousand women, with some accounts citing higher figures.

questions.q: What was Lakshmi Sahgal's maiden name before her marriage, by which she was first known in the INA?
questions.explanation: She was born Lakshmi Swaminathan and was known by that name when she joined the INA in Singapore.

questions.q: A training camp for the Rani of Jhansi Regiment was notably established in which city besides Singapore?
questions.explanation: Beyond Singapore, training camps for the regiment were also set up at Rangoon in Burma.

questions.q: The Rani of Jhansi Regiment recruits were sometimes called by what affectionate collective term reflecting their cause?
questions.explanation: Members of the regiment were commonly referred to as 'Ranis,' echoing the warrior queen the unit honoured.

questions.q: During the INA's retreat from Burma in 1945, the Ranis are remembered for what during the difficult withdrawal?
options: Marching long distances on foot under hardship
questions.explanation: During the 1945 retreat the Ranis endured a hard march on foot through Burma amid the INA's collapse.

questions.q: In what year was Lakshmi Sahgal, the regiment's commander, awarded the Padma Vibhushan by the Indian government?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi Sahgal received the Padma Vibhushan in 1998 in recognition of her service.

questions.q: The Rani of Jhansi Regiment was unusual not only for its women soldiers but also because it drew recruits across what lines?
options: Class, region and community lines among diaspora Indians
questions.explanation: Recruits came from varied class, regional and community backgrounds within the Indian diaspora, an unusually broad mix.

questions.q: Lakshmi Sahgal spent her later decades in which Indian city, continuing to practise medicine and remain politically active?
questions.explanation: After the war Lakshmi Sahgal settled in Kanpur, where she practised medicine and stayed politically engaged until her death in 2012.

questions.q: The formation of the Rani of Jhansi Regiment is historically significant chiefly because it did what?
options: Pioneered organized women's combat participation in the Indian struggle
questions.explanation: Its lasting significance lies in pioneering organized women's combat participation within the Indian independence movement.
